Name: Ampharos
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: After your opponent plays a Supporter card from his or her hand, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon. You can’t use more than 1 Jamming Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: You may discard all [L] Energy attached to Ampharos. If you do, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any Energy cards attached to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The tip of its tail shines brightly. In the olden days, people sent signals using the tail’s light.

Name: Blastoise
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach as many basic [W] Energy cards from your hand to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. If you do, your turn ends. This power can’t be used if Blastoise is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 40 damage in this way.
Flavor-Text: The jets of water it spouts from the rocket cannons on its shell can punch through thick steel.

Name: Charizard
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent has 3 or less Prize cards left, each of Charizard’s attacks does 50 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard. If tails, discard 4 Energy cards attached to Charizard. (If you can’t, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: It is said that CHARIZARD’s fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

Name: Entei
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from Entei’s attacks, discard the top 3 cards from your opponent’s deck.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Entei.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Flygon
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Flygon is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Active Pokémon between turns, excluding [F] Pokémon.
Effects: If you attach a [F] Energy card from your hand to Flygon during this turn, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It whips up sandstorms with powerful flaps of its wings. It is known as “The Desert Spirit.”

Name: Gallade
Effects: Put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until it is 50 HP away from being Knocked Out. If you do, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

You may choose as many of your face-down Prize cards as you like and put them face up. If you do, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage for each Prize card you chose. (These cards remain face up for the rest of the game.)
Flavor-Text: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

Name: Gardevoir
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your opponent’s discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent’s discard pile.) You can’t use more than 1 Telepass Poké-Power each turn. This power can’t be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, your opponent can’t use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It will try to guard its trusted Trainer with its life. It has the ability to see the future.

Name: Gastrodon East Sea
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Gastrodon West Sea in play, you may move up to 3 damage counters from Gastodon East Sea to 1 of your Gastrodon West Sea.
Effects: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Gastrodon East Sea.
Flavor-Text: It has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.

Name: Gastrodon West Sea
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Gastrodon West Sea. Then, remove 2 damage counters from Gastrodon West Sea.
Put 1 damage counter on each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: It has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.

Name: Ho-Oh
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your opponent’s turn, if Ho-Oh would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, you may flip a coin. If heads, Ho-Oh isn’t discarded. Instead, remove all damage counters, Special Conditions, and other effects from Ho-Oh. Then, discard all cards attached to Ho-Oh (except for Energy cards). This counts as Ho-Oh being Knocked Out and your opponent takes a Prize card.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of different types of basic Energy cards attached to Ho-Oh.
Flavor-Text: Its feathers are in seven colors. It is said that anyone seeing it is promised eternal happiness.

Name: Jumpluff
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever Jumpluff would be damaged by your opponent’s attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Jumpluff by that attack.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. For each heads, this attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Blown by seasonal winds, it circles the globe, scattering cotton spores as it goes.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you choose a Benched Pokémon, switch the Defending Pokémon with that Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to the Pokémon you chose. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your next turn, Lickilicky can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting to close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

Name: Ludicolo
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Ludicolo.
Effects: If you have a Stadium Card in play, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If it hears festive music, all its muscles fill with energy. It can’t help breaking out into a dance.

Name: Lugia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and return it to your opponent’s hand.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, this attack’s base damage is 40 instead of 120.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps in a deep-sea trench. If it flaps its wings, it is said to cause a 40-day storm.

Name: Mew
Effects: If you have less cards in your hand than your opponent, draw cards until you have the same number of cards as your opponent. (If you have more or the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.)

Choose an attack on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in his or her discard pile. Re-creation copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Mew performs that attack.
Flavor-Text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe MEW to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

Name: Raikou
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you attach a [L] Energy card from your hand to Raikou, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Discard 3 cards from the top of your deck. This attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each [L] Energy card you discarded. Then, attach those [L] Energy cards to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have fallen with lightning. It can fire thunderbolts from the rain clouds on its back.

Name: Roserade
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage and remove 3 damage counters from Roserade.
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.

Name: Salamence
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

Discard 2 basic [R] Energy cards or 2 basic [W] Energy cards attached to Salamence. If you discarded 2 basic [R] Energy cards, this attack does 100 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If you discarded 2 Basic [W] Energy cards, this attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (If you can’t discard cards, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: As a result of its long-held dream of flying, its cellular structure changes, and wings grew out.

Name: Suicune
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Suicune from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for up to 3 [W] Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

Name: Venusaur
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose either Asleep, Burned, or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. This power can’t be used if Venusaur is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 4 damage counters from Venusaur.
Flavor-Text: After a rainy day, the flower on its back smells stronger. The scent attracts other Pokémon.

Name: Absol
Effects: Choose a card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it. If you discarded a Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium card, choose 1 more card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.
If you played Absol from your hand during this turn, this attack’s base damage is 40 instead of 10.
Flavor-Text: It senses disasters coming and appears before people only to warn them of impending danger.

Name: Arcanine
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Arcanine from your hand to evolve 1 of your Benched Pokémon, you may switch Arcanine with 1 of your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any number of Energy cards attached to that Pokémon to Arcanine.
Effects: Arcanine does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

Name: Banette
Effects: Put as many damage counters as you like on Banette. (You can’t put more than Banette’s remaining HP.) Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
If Banette is in your discard pile, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage, then search your discard pile for Banette, show it to your opponent, and shuffle it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: A doll that became a Pokémon over its grudge from being junked. It seeks the child that disowned it.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Dugtrio during your opponent’s next turn. If Dugtrio is your Active Pokémon at the end of your opponent’s next turn, put 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its three heads moves alternately, driving it through tough soil to depths of over 60 miles.

Name: Electivire
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to Electivire. This power can’t be used if Electivire is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to Electivire. Flip a coin for each [L] Energy you discarded. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

Name: Electrode
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, if Electrode would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, you may use this power. Electrode isn’t discarded. Instead, attach it as an Energy card to 1 of your Pokémon. While attached, this card is a Special Energy card and provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.)
Effects: You may do 40 damage plus 60 more damage. If you do, Electrode does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

Name: Furret
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may switch Furret with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, move as many Energy cards attached to Furret as you like to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The mother puts its offspring to sleep by curling up around them. It corners foes with speed.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can use only that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
You may do 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Golduck is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lives in lakes. It swims faster than any human swimming champion.

Name: Golem
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

You may do 40 damage instead of 80 to the Defending Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Golem by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It sheds its hide once a year. Its boulderlike body is so tough, even dynamite can’t harm it.

Name: Jynx
Effects: If Jynx was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Move 2 damage counters from Jynx to the Defending Pokémon. If Smoochum is anywhere under Jynx, move 4 damage counters instead.
Flavor-Text: It cries sound like human speech. However, it is impossible to tell what it is trying to say.

Name: Magmortar
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: When you attach a [R] Energy card from your hand to Magmortar, remove 2 damage counters from Magmortar.
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of [R] Energy attached to Magmortar.
Does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

Name: Minun
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if any Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, you may draw 2 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Minus Charge Poké-Power each turn. This power can’t be used if Minun is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If you have Plusle on your Bench, you may move an Energy card attached to Minun to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Mothim
Effects: During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 40 more damage.
If Mothim has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by COMBEE.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin for each Nidoqueen on your Bench. This attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: If Pidgeot was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage done to Pidgeot to the Defending Pokémon.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By flapping its wings with all its might, PIDGEOT can make a gust of wind capable of bending tall trees.

Name: Plusle
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, you may search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. You can’t use more than 1 Plus Charge Poké-Power each turn. This power can’t be used if Plusle is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If you have Minun on your Bench, you may do 20 damage to any 1 Benched Pokémon instead. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

Name: Sharpedo
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Sharpedo is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Sharpedo is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage. This attack damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”

Name: Sunflora
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from each of your [G] Pokémon. You can’t use more than 1 Grass Whistle Poké-Power each turn. This power can’t be used if Sunflora is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, Sunflora is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It gets energy from warm sunlight and is known for its habit of moving in pursuit of it.

Name: Unown S
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown S, Unown E, and Unown T on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Weavile
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each of your Active Pokémon’s type is [D] until the end of your turn. If that Pokémon is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [D] Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If your opponent has 6 or more cards in his or her hand, discard a number of cards without looking until your opponent has 5 cards left in his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

Name: Wormadam Plant Cloak
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Wormadam Plant Cloak has 2 or more [G] Energy attached to it, Wormadam Plant Cloak gets +40 HP.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Wormadam Plant Cloak, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Wormadam Sandy Cloak
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, excluding damage, done to Wormadam Sandy Cloak.
Effects: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each [F] Energy attached to Wormadam Sandy Cloak.
Flavor-Text: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Wormadam Trash Cloak
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Wormadam Trash Cloak has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, any damage done to Wormadam Trash Cloak by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Wormadam Trash Cloak does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Xatu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, if Xatu is on your Bench, you may choose 1 Special Condition from 1 of your Active Pokémon and remove that Special Condition. Then, 1 of the Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition that you chose.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.

Name: Breloom
Effects: Remove from Breloom a number of damage counters equal to the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is 0, this attack does 60 damage plus 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard a [R] Energy attached to Charmeleon and this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: In the rocky mountains where CHARMELEON live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Cloyster by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.

Flip 4 coins. For each heads, choose an opponent’s Pokémon in play and this attack does 20 damage to those Pokémon. (You may choose the same Pokémon more than once.) (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.

Name: Donphan
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. You switch Donphan with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.
If the Defending Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 damage plus 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It attacks by curling up, then rolling into its foe. It can blow apart a house in one hit.

Name: Farfetch’d
Effects: During your next turn, Farfetch’d’s Leek Slap attack’s base damage is 60.
During your next turn, Farfetch’d can’t use Leek Slap.
Flavor-Text: It can’t live without the stalk it holds. That’s why it defends the stalk from attackers with its life.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, Flaaffy does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from Ivysaur.
Flavor-Text: When the bud on its back starts swelling, a sweet aroma wafts to indicate the flower’s coming bloom.

Name: Kecleon
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If any basic Energy card attached to Kecleon is the same as the Attacking Pokémon’s type, any damage done by attacks from the Pokémon to Kecleon is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does no damage to the Defending Pokémon. Instead, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can freely change its body’s color. The zigzag pattern on its belly doesn’t change, however.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Search your discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your discard pile.)
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It is highly perceptive of its Trainer’s feelings. It dances when it is feeling happy.

Name: Lombre
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
Remove 2 damage counters from Lombre.
Flavor-Text: It has a mischievous spirit. If it spots an angler, it will tug on the fishing line to interfere.

Name: Miltank
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, remove 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said that kids who drink MILTANK’s milk grow up to become hearty, healthy adults.

Name: Muk
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: At the end of each player’s turn, each of your opponent’s Active Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached to it is now Poisoned. If that Pokémon is already Poisoned, Toxic Sludge Poké-Body does nothing to that Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused and can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: A toxic fluids seeps from its body. The fluid instantly kills plants and trees on contact.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: During your next turn, each of Nidorino’s attacks does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has a violent disposition and stabs foes with its horn, which oozes poison upon impact.

Name: Pidgeotto
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It flies over its wide territory in search of prey, downing it with its highly developed claws.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack’s base damage is 10 instead of 100.
Flavor-Text: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can’t tear, it tosses far.

Name: Quagsire
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Quagsire is your Active Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, you may remove 3 damage counters from Quagsire.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has a sluggish nature. It lies at the river’s bottom, waiting for prey to stray into its mouth.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 60 more damage.
If Raticate has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It whittles its constantly growing fangs by gnawing on hard things. It can chew apart cinder walls.

Name: Roselia
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: ROSELIA raised on clean drinking water are known to grow vividly colored flowers.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose as many Trainer cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If you and your opponent have a different number of Prize cards left, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: During your next turn, Shelgon’s Protect Charge attack’s base damage is 80.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Shelgon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

Name: Skiploom
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Skiploom has any [G] Energy attached to it, any damage done to Skiploom by attacks from your opponent’s Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Switch Skiploom with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It blooms when the weather warms. It floats in the sky to soak up as much sunlight as possible.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Trace copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Smeargle performs that attack.
Flavor-Text: It marks its territory by using its tail like a paintbrush. There are more than 5,000 different marks.

Name: Smoochum
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jynx from your hand onto Smoochum (this counts as evolving Smoochum) and remove all damage counters from Smoochum.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: It tests everything by touching with its lips, which remember what it likes and dislikes.

Name: Unown K
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 2 damage counters from 1 of the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown N
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown N, Unown O, and Unown D on your Bench, you may ask your opponent to take a Prize card. If he or she does, you take a Prize card. If he or she doesn’t, draw a card.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown O
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown O, Unown N, and Unown E on your Bench and you have 1 card left in your hand, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown X on your Bench, you may look at the top card of your opponent’s deck and put it back on top of his or her deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown Z
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown Z is on your Bench and you have no cards in your deck, you may discard all cards attached to Unown Z and put Unown Z on top of your deck.
Effects: Remove as many damage counters as you like from each Unown you have in play. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Venomoth
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Venomoth is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Venomoth is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Effects: Prevent all effects of an attack, excluding damage, done to Venomoth during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It flutters its wings to scatter dustlike scales. The scales leach toxins if they contact skin.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It violently shudders its wings, generating ultrasonic waves to induce headaches in people.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Wartortle can’t use Giant Wave during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live 10,000 years. Its furry tail is popular as a symbol of longevity.

Name: Bagon
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Bagon.
Flavor-Text: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.

Name: Burmy Plant Cloak
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Plant Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic [G] Energy card and attach it to Burmy Plant Cloak.
Effects: If Burmy Plant Cloak has any [G] Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

Name: Burmy Sandy Cloak
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Sandy Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic [F] Energy card and attach it to Burmy Sandy Cloak.
Effects: If Burmy Sandy Cloak has any [F] Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

Name: Burmy Trash Cloak
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Trash Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic [M] Energy card and attach it to Burmy Trash Cloak.
Effects: If Burmy Trash Cloak has any [M] Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

Name: Carvanha
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.
Flavor-Text: They can swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

Name: Clefairy
Move-Energy:
Held Item ⇢ Moon Stone
 Clefairy can evolve during the turn you play it.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Corsola
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 different types of Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Many live in the clean seas of the south. They apparently can’t live in polluted waters.

Name: Diglett
Effects: Draw a card from the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lives underground. Because of its dark habitat, it is repelled by bright sunlight.

Name: Duskull
Move-Energy:
Held Item ⇢ Reaper Cloth
 Duskull can evolve during the turn you play it.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If tails, Electabuzz does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.

Name: Grimer
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It was born when sludge in a dirty stream was exposed to the moon’s X-rays. It appears among filth.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

Name: Hoppip
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Hoppip has any [G] Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Hoppip is 0.
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions from Hoppip.
Flavor-Text: It drifts on winds. It is said that when HOPPIP gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Instead of hands, it uses its tongue, which is twice its height. Its sticky saliva grips anything.

Name: Lotad
Effects: If Lotad has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It looks like an aquatic plant and serves as a ferry to Pokémon that can’t swim.

Name: Magmar
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Born in the spout of a volcano, its body is covered by flames that shimmer like the sun.

Name: Mareep
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy coat swells to double when static electricity builds up. Touching it can be shocking.

Name: Murkrow
Move-Energy:
Held Item ⇢ Dusk Stone
 Murkrow can evolve during the turn you play it.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

Name: Natu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Look at the top 5 cards of either player’s deck and put them back on top of that player’s deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: It picks food from cactus plants, deftly avoiding buds and spines. It seems to skip about to move.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It scans its surroundings by raising its ears out of the grass. Its toxic horn is for protection.

Name: Phanpy
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Phanpy.
Phanpy does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: If Psyduck is Confused, draw 2 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, Psyduck is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If its usual headache worsens, its starts exhibiting odd powers. It can’t remember doing so, however.

Name: Qwilfish
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your opponent’s turn, if Qwilfish is your Active Pokémon and is damage by an attack (even if Qwilfish is Knocked Out), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It shoots the poison spines on its body in all directions. Its round form makes it a poor swimmer.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It uses the thorns on its head to sense human emotions. It is said to appear in front of cheerful people.

Name: Rattata
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Cautious in the extreme, its hardy vitality lets it live in any kind of environment.

Name: Sentret
Effects: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a very nervous nature. It stands up high on its tail so it can scan wide areas.

Name: Shellder
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Shellder.
Flavor-Text: It swims backwards by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.

Name: Shellos East Sea
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Shellos West Sea and Shellos East Sea and put them on your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its colors and shapes differ from region to region. In the Sinnoh region, two types are confirmed.

Name: Shellos West Sea
Effects: Discard a [W] Energy attached to Shellos West Sea and remove all damage counters from Shellos West Sea.
Flavor-Text: Its colors and shapes differ from region to region. In the Sinnoh region, two types are confirmed.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

Name: Shuckle
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Shuckle by your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Special Energy cards attached to it.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. As long as the Defending Pokémon remains Poisoned by this attack, it can’t use any Poké-Body.
Flavor-Text: It stores berries in its shell. The berries eventually ferment to become delicious juices.

Name: Shuppet
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

Name: Spinda
Effects: Draw a card from the top and the bottom of your deck.
If any basic Energy card attached to Spinda is the same type as any Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: No two Spinda have the same pattern of spots. Its tottering step fouls the aim of foes.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to Squirtle. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

Name: Stantler
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon isn’t an Evolved Pokémon, that Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Staring at its antlers creates an odd sensation as if one were being drawn into their centers.

Name: Sunkern
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove all damage counters from Sunkern, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from Sunkern, and put it onto Sunkern. (This counts as evolving Sunkern.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It suddenly falls out of the sky in the morning. A year after a cold summer, their population explodes.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It makes a conical pit in desert sand and lies in wait at the bottom for prey to come tumbling down.

Name: Venonat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At nights, its kind is drawn by light.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Voltorb does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

Name: Wooper
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It lives in cold water, half burying itself in mud at the bottom to sleep.

Name: Bebe’s Search
Description: Choose a card from your hand and put it on top of your deck. Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. (If this is the only card in your hand, you can’t play this card.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Night Maintenance
Description: Search your discard pile for up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

Name: PlusPower
Description: Attach PlusPower to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your turn.If the Pokémon PlusPower is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Professor Oak’s Visit
Description: Draw 3 cards. Then, choose a card from your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Professor Rowan
Description: Choose 1 card in your hand and shuffle the rest of your cards into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. (If this is the only card in your hand, you can’t play this card.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Rival
Description: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. Your opponent chooses 3 of those cards. Put those cards into your hand and put other 2 cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Roseanne’s Research
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Basic Pokémon and basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Team Galactic’s Mars
Description: Draw 2 cards. Then, choose a card from your opponent’s hand without looking and put it on the bottom of his or her deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Potion
Description: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Darkness Energy
Description: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn’t [D]. Darkness Energy provides [D] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Metal Energy
Description: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn’t [M]. Metal Energy provides [M] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Gardevoir LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), choose 1 of your Active Pokémon or 1 or your Benched Pokémon and switch Gardevoir with that Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 1 Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s) with the fewest remaining HP (excluding Gardevoir) and that Pokémon is now Knocked Out.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Gardevoir. Gardevoir LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Honchkrow LV.X
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from this attack, you may search your discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Honchkrow. Honchkrow LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.
